They spend an hour with “The Human Eye: An Anatomical Atlas” by John D. P. M. G. de Kruif, meticulously annotating cross-sections of the globe. They color-code each layer: neural retina, RPE, Bruch’s membrane, choroid, ciliary body, and IPE. They draw arrows indicating fluid dynamics (aqueous humor flow), ion transport (Na+/K+ ATPase in IPE), and paracrine signaling (PEDF → photoreceptors). This is their ritual for consolidating spatial understanding of ocular anatomy.
